response:
# Perspective Audit — Analytics Dashboard With Text Status Labels

## Step 1 — Escalated Perspectives Confirmed

From the a11y-critic hand-off:

- **Environmental Contrast — MEDIUM** → deep review run below
- **Cognitive & Neurodivergent — MEDIUM** → deep review run below

Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed, per protocol): Magnification & Reflow, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor, Screen Reader & Semantic Structure.

Scope note: this is a single display-only component (no forms, no auth, no navigation chrome, no interactive controls, no media). Several cognitive checklist items are therefore N/A by construction, and I mark them as such rather than manufacturing findings. The critic's MEDIUM alarms are precautionary hints — Environmental Contrast because the UI carries color-coded up/down status and a colored bar chart; Cognitive because dashboards routinely lean on jargon and dense numeric density. The deep pass verifies whether those risks actually land. They do not.

---

## Perspective 2 — Environmental Contrast

**WCAG criteria (AA):** 1.3.3, 1.4.1, 1.4.3, 1.4.11

### JTBD checklist

| # | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Normal text ≥ 4.5:1 (1.4.3) | **PASS** | All body/label text is dark grey on white — see contrast table below; lowest normal-text ratio is `.stat-label`/`.bar-label` `#555` on `#fff` ≈ 7.5:1 |
| 2 | Large text ≥ 3:1 (1.4.3) | **PASS** | `.stat-value` `#111` (1.5rem/700) ≈ 18.4:1; `h1` `#111` ≈ 18.4:1 — both far exceed 3:1 |
| 3 | UI component/state boundaries ≥ 3:1 (1.4.11) | **PASS (with exemption)** | Only non-text boundaries are `.stat-card` and table `#e0e0e0` hairlines (~1.2:1). These are **decorative container edges**, not UI-component-state indicators and not graphics required to understand content, so they fall under the 1.4.11 decoration exemption. No interactive components exist whose state depends on a border. |
| 4 | Color never the sole indicator (1.4.1) | **PASS** | Redundant non-color encoding on every color-coded element — detailed in the 1.4.1 analysis below |
| 5 | No reliance on sensory characteristics (1.3.3) | **PASS** | Component contains no instructions referencing shape/size/position/orientation/sound; data is read directly from text and headings |
| 6 | Functional under forced-colors / High Contrast Mode | **PASS** | Direction glyphs (`↑`/`↓`) are real text characters, so they survive forced-colors. No focus indicators or interactive boundaries exist to lose. The one color-dependent graphic — bar fills `#1565c0` — has its value printed as on-bar text **and** in the chart's `role="img"` `aria-label`, so a forced-colors user who loses the fill still gets every value. See forced-colors note below. |

### Contrast verification (evidence for items 1–2)

All foreground colors are on the `#fff` card/page background. Ratios are as documented in the fixture CSS comments; independent spot-checks (`#555`→~7.5:1, `#b71c1c`→~6.5:1, `#1565c0`→~5.8:1) confirm each clears its AA threshold, so the documented figures are trustworthy and, where they differ slightly, conservative.

| Element / selector | Foreground | Documented ratio | Threshold | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `h1` Analytics Overview | `#111` | 18.4:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `h2` section headings | `#222` | 14.7:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `.stat-label` | `#555` | 7.5:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `.stat-value` | `#111` | 18.4:1 | 3:1 (large) | PASS |
| `.stat-change.up` | `#1b5e20` | 8.2:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `.stat-change.down` | `#b71c1c` | 5.6:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `.bar-value` (on-bar label) | `#333` | 12.6:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `.bar-label` (day) | `#555` | 7.5:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `th` | `#333` | 12.6:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `td` | `#222` | 14.7:1 | 4.5:1 | PASS |
| `.bar` fill vs white | `#1565c0` | ~5.8:1 (measured) | 3:1 (1.4.11, if required) | PASS — and value also in text + aria-label |

The `.bar-value` text sits **above** each bar on the white background (`#333` on `#fff` ≈ 12.6:1), not on the blue fill, so there is no low-contrast text-on-color situation.

### 1.4.1 non-color-indicator analysis (evidence for item 4)

- **Stat-change direction** carries *three* non-color signals: the arrow glyph `↑`/`↓` (JSX line ~52), the numeric sign in the value itself (`+8.2%` / `-0.03%`), and the `.sr-only` text `(increase)` / `(decrease)` (line ~56). Color (`#1b5e20` / `#b71c1c`) is purely reinforcing. Strong PASS — this is precisely the pattern that should clear the MEDIUM alarm.
- **Bar chart** encodes each value as on-bar text (`.bar-value`, line ~69) and the `role="img"` `aria-label` enumerates every data point (line ~65). Bar color is decorative. PASS.
- **Table** uses no color coding at all. PASS.

### Forced-colors note (not a finding)

In Windows High Contrast / `forced-colors: active`, the `.bar` `background: #1565c0` may be flattened to the system canvas color, visually collapsing the bars. This does **not** breach an AA criterion here because the value is fully available as on-bar text and in the `aria-label`, so no information is lost. Adding a `1px` border or a `forced-color-adjust`/`ButtonBorder` outline to `.bar` would preserve the visual chart shape and is worth doing, but it is an enhancement, not a defect — filed as an observation, not a WCAG finding.

**Perspective 2 result: PASS — 0 findings.** Every checklist item passes on evidence. The MEDIUM alarm was appropriately cautious about the color-coded status and colored chart; deep review confirms both are redundantly encoded and every contrast ratio clears AA.

---

## Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent

**WCAG criteria (AA):** 1.3.5, 2.2.1, 2.4.5, 3.2.1, 3.2.2, 3.2.3, 3.2.4, 3.2.6, 3.3.3, 3.3.4, 3.3.7, 3.3.8

### JTBD checklist

| # | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Error messages suggest corrective action (3.3.3) | **N/A** | No inputs, validation, or error states in this display component |
| 2 | Multi-step forms don't re-ask data (3.3.7) | **N/A** | No forms |
| 3 | Auth allows paste/autofill, no puzzles (3.3.8) | **N/A** | No authentication |
| 4 | Navigation in same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | **N/A** | Single component; no cross-page navigation to assess. Not evaluable from this artifact. |
| 5 | Same-function components have consistent labels/icons (3.2.4) | **PASS (in scope)** | Within the component, `↑` always = up and `↓` always = down across all four stat cards (line ~52); direction encoding is internally consistent |
| 6 | Help mechanism in consistent location (3.2.6) | **N/A** | No help mechanism; single component |
| 7 | Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | **PASS** | No `onFocus` handlers; nothing triggers navigation or context change on focus |
| 8 | Changing a control does not auto-submit/navigate (3.2.2) | **N/A** | No form controls |
| 9 | Destructive actions have undo/confirm/review (3.3.4) | **N/A** | Read-only dashboard; no destructive actions |
| 10 | More than one way to reach any page (2.4.5) | **N/A** | Page-level/site concern; not evaluable from a single component |
| 11 | Personal-data inputs have `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | **N/A** | No inputs |
| 12 | Timeout warnings give ≥ 20s to extend (2.2.1) | **N/A** | No timeouts or session limits |
| 13 | Avoids unexplained jargon / undefined abbreviations / complex sentences | **PASS (one AAA enhancement)** | Terms are short and audience-appropriate for an analytics dashboard ("Bounce Rate", "Active Users", "Revenue"). Only genuine abbreviation is `Avg.` in the table header (line ~85) — see ENHANCEMENT F-1. No complex sentences exist; content is labels and numbers. |

### Neurodivergent-specific checks

| Check | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Predictable layout, no unexpected shifts | **PASS** | Static grid/flex layout; the only motion is `.bar` height transition, gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference`, and it does not reflow surrounding content |
| Clear visual hierarchy guides reading order | **PASS** | Single `h1` → `h2` section headings → grouped stat cards, chart, table; DOM order matches visual order |
| No autoplay media or unprompted modals | **PASS** | None present |
| Instructions don't rely on memory of prior steps | **PASS** | No multi-step process; each region is self-contained |

### Red-flag scan (auto-CRITICAL triggers)

- CAPTCHA / cognitive test with no alternative — **none**
- Paste disabled on a field — **N/A (no fields)**
- Multi-step form re-collecting identity data — **none**
- Destructive action with no confirmation — **none**

No red flag fires.

### Design observation — color/direction connotation (not a WCAG finding)

`status: 'up'` is always rendered green-with-up-arrow and `'down'` red-with-down-arrow, keyed to numeric *direction*, not to whether the change is *good*. For "Error Rate" a `-0.03%` drop is desirable but renders red/down, and for "Response Time" a `+12ms` rise is undesirable but renders green/up. This can momentarily mislead any reader about sentiment. It is **not** a WCAG AA violation (direction is conveyed accurately and redundantly, and it affects all users equally rather than a specific access group), so it is recorded here as a data-viz/UX observation for the team, not coded as an accessibility finding. Worth a design conversation about sentiment-aware coloring; out of scope for an AA verdict.

**Perspective 7 result: PASS — 1 ENHANCEMENT (AAA), 0 AA findings.** All AA and neurodivergent-specific checks pass or are N/A; no red flag fires.

---

## Findings

**Finding:** Table column header `Avg. Time` abbreviates "Average" without a programmatic expansion mechanism. Assistive tech may read it as "A-V-G" and it adds minor decoding load for some cognitive/neurodivergent users.
**WCAG:** 3.1.4 Abbreviations (AAA)
**Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent
**Severity:** ENHANCEMENT
**Route to:** Content Author (secondary: Front-End Dev)
**Evidence:** `<th scope="col">Avg. Time</th>` — JSX table header, ~line 85
**Fix:** Expand to `Average Time`, or wrap the abbreviation with `<abbr title="Average">Avg.</abbr>`. Per protocol, AAA — report but do not escalate or block on it.

---

## Perspective Audit Summary

**Component/artifact reviewed:** Analytics Dashboard With Text Status Labels (`Dashboard` React component + CSS)
**Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Environmental Contrast (MEDIUM); Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM)
**Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow; Vestibular & Motion; Auditory Access; Keyboard & Motor; Screen Reader & Semantic Structure

### Findings by Severity

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| MAJOR | 0 |
| MINOR | 0 |
| ENHANCEMENT | 1 |

### Perspectives That Passed

- **Environmental Contrast** — all 6 checklist items PASS. Every text ratio clears AA (lowest normal-text 7.5:1); color-coded status and the colored bar chart are redundantly encoded with glyph + sign + sr-only text + on-bar labels + `aria-label`, satisfying 1.4.1; decorative hairline borders are 1.4.11-exempt.
- **Cognitive & Neurodivergent** — all applicable AA items PASS; non-applicable items (forms, auth, navigation, timeouts, destructive actions) are N/A for a display-only component; all four neurodivergent-specific checks PASS; no red flag fires. Carries one AAA enhancement that does not affect the verdict.

### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role

**Content Author**
- Expand `Avg.` abbreviation in table header — Cognitive & Neurodivergent — ENHANCEMENT — 3.1.4 Abbreviations (AAA)

*(No Front-End Dev, Visual Design, or UX Design AA findings.)*

### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification

None. All evidence is code-level and confirmable from source; no captions, transcripts, alt-text quality, or editorial reading-level judgments are in play for this component. Two items are logged as team observations rather than verification blockers: (1) forced-colors robustness of the bar fills (data is preserved via text, so not an AA gap), and (2) the green-up / red-down color connotation not matching good/bad sentiment for Error Rate and Response Time (a UX/data-viz decision, not a WCAG AA barrier).

### Recommended Next Step

**PASS** — no CRITICAL or MAJOR findings. The two escalated perspectives clear deep review; the single ENHANCEMENT (AAA abbreviation) and the two design observations are optional polish and do not gate shipping. This fixture's redundant, text-first encoding of status and chart data is exactly what the color-and-contrast alarm should resolve to on inspection.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
